figures.caption: Fig. 4. Effect of the number of input views on GraspNet grasping performance (AP).

Fig. 1. Pipeline of MG-Grasp: given sparse posed RGB images, we first perform Depth Aggregation and Dense Correspondence to obtain up-to-scale pointmaps, and then recover metric scale via Triangulation-based Scale Recovery. The metric pointmaps are further refined with multi-view consistency optimization to produce consistent geometry, which is finally used for grasp guidance and 6-DoF grasp generation.
TABLE I RESULTS ON GRASPNET DATASET, SHOWING AP ON THE REALSENSE/KINECT SPLITS RESPECTIVELY
Fig. 3. Comparison of unprojected point clouds produced by different geometry pipelines. The highlighted regions illustrate misaligned surface.
